next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 15

The Margins smelled like exposure. Hot stone, alkali, old water pulled too far underground. Six weeks ago Sable would have called it dirt and moved on. Now the heat lay on her scalp, the backs of her hands, the strip of skin above her collar where the Vault's formal neckline had given way, three weeks earlier, to an unlaced opening she no longer noticed.

Two hundred and thirty-eight sat at the edge of her awareness. She checked it the way she used to check her schedule, automatically, while doing something else. The schedule had never shifted. The number did, and every drop carried the same contradiction: less of what she had been, more of whatever she was becoming.

The settlement was eight structures around a collapsed well. Her route had passed east of it along a former Hoarded supply road, now just packed earth with old cart grooves baked hard in the sun. The detour happened because a man sat outside the largest house sorting grain one kernel at a time, and the rhythm of his hands was wrong.

Sable knew that rhythm. She had seen it in Council chambers, in administrative offices, in Lord Aldraste reaching for a glass and arriving there late.

The man's fingers moved, stopped, moved again. Three kernels. A pause. Two more. A longer pause.

Sable sat on the low stone wall across from him. "You're Hoarded."

He looked up. The eyes found her a beat after the sound of her voice. Once they did, the person behind them was still sharp.

"Was," he said. "Fifteen years."

"What level did you leave at?"

"Three hundred." He went back to the grain. "I know what you're...
open_hooks_to_carry: Second consecutive Kem chapter — POV alternation broken; Vel's inner world goes silent
Communication collapsed to logistics; mechanical cooperation that works despite broken trust
Margins settlement rescue: collapsed mining substrate, family trapped — Vel directs, Kem executes with 6 levels of precise spending (31 to 25)
Dark comedy of the rescue: precision teamwork performed in emotional silence; bystander asks if they're partners, neither answers

Kem's flash of comprehension: exhausted at 25, he briefly understands why someone would choose never to feel any of this — the terror of full embodiment, the Hoarded impulse toward preservation. Not agreement. Comprehension. Then he looks at the rescued family and it passes.
Kem's decision to continue: not survival math, not new information — he started spending for other people and that person doesn't stop at 25
Kem's signet: a Margins child asks about it; Kem explains and is ashamed
Vel consults Maren's route journal; Kem notices the handwriting isn't hers and the care she shows it
prev_tail_excerpt:
...at eighteen, Dren with one bad arm, and the only route any of them had. Leaving was a plan for people who knew where they were going.

Kem looked down at his right hand instead. The signet sat on his ring finger, silver-dark, the closed circle crest worn smooth by weeks of sweat and dust. House Aldraste. Preservation by standing still while everyone else paid.

He pulled it off.

Pale skin showed underneath, soft where the metal had sealed itself against the world. The ring fit in his palm with ugly familiarity. For a second he pictured throwing it over the wall into the scrub and hearing nothing when it landed.

Instead he closed his hand and put it in his pocket.

Dren came back before either of them spoke again. "South road's passable if we cut the fields for a stretch. Another collapse ahead. Dry channel under it."

"We move now," Vel said.

Kem shouldered his pack first. A new callus along his palm caught on the strap and made him notice, again, that his hands no longer looked like they belonged to someone from the Vault. Ten feet out into the lane he stopped just long enough for the others to fall in, then kept going.

Dren took the middle without comment. Vel stayed behind him. No one argued the formation.

Another quarter mile and the road broke again. They climbed down into the dry drainage, dirt sliding under their boots, old earth walls close on either side, the sky narrowed to a hard white strip overhead. Thirty-one hummed at the edge of Kem's awareness. Ahead of him, Dren's pack shifted with each step. Behind him, Vel kept the distance he set.

He kept walking.

setups_to_plant.description: Kem's brief understanding of the Hoarded impulse — the terror of embodiment — is a moment of complexity that complicates the novel's thesis without endorsing preservation. Filed for potential Book 2 resonance.

state_deltas_required: The fractured alliance forced into mechanical cooperation; the cost of embodiment at near-zero levels
Kem chooses to continue for non-transactional reasons; mechanical competence demonstrated despite emotional fracture; Kem briefly comprehends the Hoarded impulse toward preservation and rejects it
